axis: host hexa shim refresh — route to ω-runtime-1 equipped interp
trigger: Phase B agent flagged: ~/.hx/bin/hexa routes to old build/hexa_interp lacking pipe_*

investigation.real_hexa_resolved_to: /Users/ghost/.hx/packages/hexa/hexa (symlink target /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/hexa) → exec build/hexa.real (367376 byte Mach-O dispatcher) → spawns build/hexa_interp (the OLD 2761872 byte Mach-O — sha256 1b7ba9dc6eca3d398a3bb09a1d942b5a126da69b4c23cdba47542f50d08ceadc, mtime 2026-04-25T14:02)

binary_inventory.kind: Mach-O arm64 dispatcher (refs build/hexa_interp by path)

investigation.mismatch_root_cause: Two parallel dispatcher chains exist: (1) hexa.real (Mach-O dispatcher, hard-coded reference 'build/hexa_interp') spawns build/hexa_interp by path, NOT build/hexa_interp.real. The Phase A rebuild this session wrote ONLY hexa_interp.real (the canonical artifact name) and did not refresh build/hexa_interp. (2) ~/.hx/packages/hexa is a symlink to /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang, so refreshing the same build/ dir fixes both bin/hexa-resolver→hexa.real path and any direct /core/hexa-lang/hexa invocation. Net: build/hexa_interp was the stale artifact eaten by hexa.real.

files_changed_or_replaced: /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp.bak.omega-toolchain-1_20260426 (NEW backup of pre-fix Apr-25 binary, sha256 1b7ba9d…)
/Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp (REPLACED — was 2761872-byte stale Mach-O, now 16-byte symlink → hexa_interp.real)
fix_applied.method_rationale: First attempt was cp -p hexa_interp.real → hexa_interp; binary content identical (sha256 match) but macOS taskgated/code-sig cache keyed on file path → freshly-named copy received SIGKILL (rc=137) on every invocation while the .real-named original ran fine. Switched to symlink so the kernel resolves to the already-blessed inode/path and inherits its working signature state. Symlink survives resolver/dispatcher chain unchanged — exec() follows it transparently.

before.stderr: Runtime error: undefined function: pipe_spawn / pipe_alive / pipe_close

after.test_command: HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 /Users/ghost/.hx/bin/hexa /tmp/pipe_spawn_test.hexa  AND  /Users/ghost/.hx/bin/hexa /tmp/pipe_spawn_test.hexa (full resolver, darwin-bypass marker route)

validation.container_proof: docker exec hexa-exec /usr/local/bin/hexa /Users/ghost/pipe_spawn_test.hexa → alive=true done rc=0 (image's /usr/local/bin/hexa Linux ELF independent of host build/hexa_interp; not touched)

stale_artifacts_observed: Total: ~30 backup variants — none on PATH; eat ~50MB disk; no current behavioral risk; cleanup deferred to a janitor cycle
